Title: Steroidal alkaloids defence metabolism and plant growth are modulated by the joint action of gibberellin and jasmonate signalling

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strongly upregulated in myc1 and reduced in MYC2-RNAi
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SMT1 expression marks partitioning between phytosterol and SGA precursor metabolism; MYC1 represses its promoter
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SMT1 expression rose about six-fold in myc1 and fell in MYC2-RNAi. 2. MYC1 suppressed reporter expression from the SMT1 upstream region. 3. myc1 accumulated more sitosterol and isofucosterol, indicating sterol-branch redirection associated with reduced SGA state.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering SMT1-associated flux affects phytosterol composition and may influence growth
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential flux-partitioning lever between phytosterols and SGAs, but likely pleiotropic
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a common gene because paper emphasizes branch control rather than direct catalytic dissection here.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: jasmonate biosynthesis influencing steroidal glycoalkaloid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: jasmonate formation upstream of JA-Ile signalling
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: allene oxide cyclase, CYP74-independent oxylipin pathway enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes unstable allene oxide to form 12-oxo-phytodienoic acid in jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies upstream hormone signal required for activation of target-product biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced endogenous JA biosynthesis lowers basal α-tomatine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC-RNAi leaves contained about two-fold lower basal α-tomatine than wild type. 2. MeJA restored induction but AOC-RNAi did not exceed untreated WT levels. 3. AOC-dependent JA biosynthesis is required for full constitutive SGA production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi silencing; metabolite profiling by LC-MS; qRT-PCR after MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced JA biosynthesis is associated with lower defence metabolite accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics used in study overall; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modulating JA biosynthesis can tune constitutive SGA defence levels, but may compromise defence capacity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AOC was experimentally manipulated as a causal JA-biosynthetic component affecting SGA output.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: jasmonate-Ile biosynthesis influencing steroidal glycoalkaloid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: conjugates jasmonic acid to isoleucine to form bioactive JA-Ile
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies active hormone signal required for target-product biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of JAR1 reduces JA-Ile and lowers basal α-tomatine and related SGAs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR jar1 leaves showed severe reduction in JA-Ile. 2. Basal α-tomatine and other SGAs were reduced in jar1. 3. MeJA still induced some α-tomatine, likely because of partial redundancy among tomato JAR genes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 loss-of-function; hormone quantification; LC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key upstream activation step for JA signalling in this context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing JA-Ile formation decreases defence metabolite production
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabidopsis U6-26 promoter used for CRISPR sgRNAs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modulate JA-dependent SGA accumulation, though redundancy among JAR genes should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tomato contains nine JAR genes, so single-gene disruption gives partial phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: cholesterol precursor supply for SGA branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyses a sterol side-chain reduction step contributing to cholesterol-derived precursor supply for SGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by MeJA, PAC, gid1TRI, and in pro after MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression of SSR2 positively associates with elevated α-tomatine accumulation under JA activation and reduced GA signalling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSR2 transcript was induced by MeJA and PAC treatments. 2. SSR2 expression increased in gid1TRI and its protein abundance was elevated there. 3. Reduced expression in some JA/GA-defective backgrounds correlated with lower SGA output.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; proteomics in gid1TRI; correlation with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect sterol homeostasis in addition to SGA production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate precursor-supply gene for engineering elevated SGAs, but sterol-related pleiotropy should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a representative sterol precursor gene tightly responsive to JA/GA crosstalk.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SGA pathway enzymatic step contributing to α-tomatine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by MeJA, PAC, gid1TRI and MYC/GAME9 transactivation context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA; PAC; altered JA and GA signalling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GAME11 expression/protein abundance positively tracks α-tomatine accumulation across JA/GA perturbations
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME11 was reduced in AOC-RNAi and jar1 backgrounds with reduced SGA. 2. GAME11 was induced by MeJA and PAC and elevated in gid1TRI. 3. MYC1/MYC2 together with GAME9 activated the GAME11 upstream region.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native uprGAME11 tested in reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter or coding sequence could be used in pathway engineering for higher constitutive/induced SGA production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific enzymatic chemistry not described in provided text, but gene is a clear core SGA biosynthetic target.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SGA pathway enzymatic step contributing to α-tomatine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by MeJA; reduced in jar1 and pro; elevated transcript in gid1TRI though protein not significantly increased there
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA; PAC; altered JA and GA signalling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GAME4 expression generally associates positively with α-tomatine biosynthesis under JA/GA control
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME4 transcript was reduced in jar1 and pro and induced by MeJA. 2. GAME4 protein was reduced in jai1 and not significantly upregulated in gid1TRI unlike other SGA enzymes. 3. MYC2 alone or with GAME9 activated the GAME4 upstream region.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native uprGAME4 tested in reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targetable SGA enzyme responsive to hormone crosstalk, but promoter regulation differs from GAME11
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter activation appears more dependent on MYC2 than MYC1 in reporter assays.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SGA pathway enzymatic step contributing to α-tomatine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by MeJA, PAC, jar1+MeJA, and gid1TRI; protein elevated in gid1TRI
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA; PAC; altered JA and GA signalling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GAME12 is positively associated with α-tomatine accumulation under JA activation and reduced GA perception
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME12 transcript was induced by MeJA in multiple genetic backgrounds. 2. PAC and gid1TRI also enhanced GAME12 expression. 3. GAME12 protein abundance increased in gid1TRI, matching elevated α-tomatine.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate core pathway gene responsive to hormonal control for increasing SGA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a consistent responsive SGA biosynthetic enzyme in JA/GA perturbations.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts bioactive GA1 and GA4 into inactive GA8 and GA34
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts flux away from competing growth hormone branch, thereby favoring target-product accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by MYC2 overexpression; strongly downregulated in myc1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MYC-mediated activation of GA2ox5 lowers active GA and supports higher α-tomatine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC2 overexpression induced GA2ox5, whereas myc1 strongly reduced it. 2. MYC1 and MYC2 activated the GA2ox5 promoter in transient assays. 3. DELLA alleviated JAZ1/JAZ2 repression of MYC-driven GA2ox5 promoter activation, linking GA catabolism to SGA control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase transactivation; hormonal quantification; interaction-modulation assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for active GA depletion in this study context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced GA catabolism may reduce growth
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native uprGA2ox5 tested in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: regulated by MYC1; MYC2; modulation by JAZ1; JAZ2; DELLA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target to shift balance from growth toward defence metabolite accumulation, though dwarfing risk is high
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene is central to mechanistic JA-GA crosstalk described in the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts GA precursors GA12 and GA53 into inactive GA110 and GA97
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts flux away from active GA production, thereby favoring target-product accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by MYC2 overexpression; reduced in MYC2-RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MYC-mediated induction of GA2ox7 contributes to lower GA and supports α-tomatine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC2 overexpression induced GA2ox7, while MYC2-RNAi reduced it. 2. MYC1 and MYC2 activated the GA2ox7 promoter in transient assays. 3. This links JA-responsive MYCs to GA precursor catabolism and defence-growth tradeoff.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase transactivation; GA metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for precursor depletion in this study context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced GA catabolism may restrict elongation growth
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native uprGA2ox7 tested in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for breeding lower-GA/high-SGA phenotypes if growth penalty is acceptable
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes prior literature on tai-1/ga2ox7 role in internode elongation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyses GA biosynthetic oxidation steps leading toward bioactive gibberellin production
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes with target-product branch by promoting growth hormone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated in myc1; MYC1 inhibits promoter activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher GA20ox2 expression associates with increased active GA and reduced SGA-favoring defence state
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of MYC1 caused upregulation of GA20ox2. 2. MYC1 inhibited the GA20ox2 promoter in transient assays. 3. This indicates MYC1 suppresses GA biosynthesis while promoting SGA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; transient transactivation; GA quantification association
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key GA biosynthetic control step in study model
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing GA20ox2 may cause growth suppression
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native uprGA20ox2 tested in reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential editing target to weaken growth-promoting GA biosynthesis and enhance defence chemistry, with expected dwarfing tradeoff
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is directly positioned in the MYC-controlled JA-GA-SGA crosstalk mechanism.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: JA-Ile perception leading to JAZ repressor removal and activation of downstream transcription
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of SGAs via JA signalling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: JAZ repressors; downstream SGA biosynthetic program including SSR2, GAME11, GAME4, GAME12
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SGA biosynthetic enzyme accumulation indirectly through JA signalling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: COI1 transcript decreased after MeJA in some treatments; jai1 mutant shows broadly reduced downstream gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of JAI1 reduces basal α-tomatine and downregulates SGA biosynthetic enzymes/proteins
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential JA perception component for activation of SGA metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: analysis of jai1 mutant; qRT-PCR; proteomics; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. jai1 has lower basal α-tomatine than WT. 2. Most JA signalling and SGA biosynthesis genes are strongly reduced in jai1. 3. SGA biosynthetic proteins SSR2, GAME11, GAME4 and GAME12 are downregulated in jai1.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced JA perception lowers defence metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating COI1 strongly alters defence metabolites but is likely too pleiotropic for practical use
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper refers to mutant as jai1, functionally the tomato COI1 receptor.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression via sequestration of MYC factors
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SGA biosynthesis and JA feedback regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses MYC-mediated activation of SGA and GA-catabolism targets
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: negative feedback loop because MYCs induce JAZ expression and JAZ1 represses MYCs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts with MYC1 and MYC2 in split-LUC; repression alleviated by DELLA in transactivation assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in leaves; induced by MeJA and some combined PAC+MeJA treatments
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression reduces α-tomatine, indicating JAZ1 is a negative regulator of SGA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent repressor of constitutive and induced SGA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression and RNAi lines; LC-MS metabolite profiling; Y2H/split-LUC; promoter activation assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ1 overexpression reduced α-tomatine by about two- to three-fold. 2. JAZ1-RNAi increased α-tomatine. 3. JAZ1 interacts with MYC1/MYC2 and represses their activation of GA2ox5 promoter activity.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing JAZ1 may enhance defence metabolites and possibly growth penalties via stronger JA responses
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S-like overexpression implied; specific promoter not stated
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A repressor target for derepressing SGA defence chemistry, though broader JA-response effects are expected
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although earlier work suggested JAZ1 does not interact with JAI1, it clearly affects SGA metabolism here.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SGA biosynthesis and JA feedback regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: JA-Ile/COI1 signalling; MYC1/MYC2 transcriptional activation of uprJAZ2
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts with JAI1, NINJA, MYC1 and MYC2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in leaves; induced by combined PAC+MeJA; promoter activated by MYC1 and MYC2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: JAZ2 overexpression reduces α-tomatine, supporting a repressor role in SGA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative regulator within JA-SGA feedback module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression line; LC-MS metabolite profiling; Y2H/split-LUC; transactivation assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ2 overexpression lowered α-tomatine. 2. MYC1 and MYC2 activated the JAZ2 promoter. 3. JAZ2 repressed MYC1/MYC2 activation of GA2ox5 reporter, and DELLA attenuated this repression.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lowering JAZ2 may increase constitutive JA outputs and growth cost
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native uprJAZ2 tested; overexpression promoter not stated
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible derepression target, particularly in stacked engineering with MYCs/GAME9
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the triad of JAZ repressors highlighted by the paper.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SGA biosynthesis and JA feedback regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses MYC-mediated activation of SGA pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts with JAI1, NINJA, MYC1 and MYC2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: leaf-expressed; strongly induced in jar1, MYC2-OE, PAC treatment, and combined PAC+MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: JAZ6 RNAi increases α-tomatine, demonstrating negative regulation of SGA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative regulator in leaf SGA control
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi line; LC-MS metabolite profiling; Y2H/split-LUC; promoter activation assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ6-RNAi increased α-tomatine. 2. JAZ6 transcript was one of the most induced JA feedback markers in several conditions. 3. MYC2, but not MYC1, activated the JAZ6 promoter, indicating partial specialization.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing JAZ6 may strengthen JA defence outputs and impair growth
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native uprJAZ6 tested; RNAi construct promoter not stated
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Selective repression release via JAZ6 could enhance SGA defence chemistry
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: JAZ6 is one of the strongest feedback-responsive repressors in this system.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation and repression depending on target promoter; protein-protein interaction with JAZs and DELLA
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SGA biosynthesis; GA catabolism/signalling; sterol-branch partitioning
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GAME11; JAZ2; GA2ox5; GA2ox7; DELLA; SMT1 (repression); GA20ox2 (repression)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SGA and GA-catabolism/signalling genes; represses SMT1 and GA20ox2
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: JA signalling relieved from JAZ repression; PAC/GA crosstalk via DELLA
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in negative feedback by activating JAZ2
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts with JAZ1; JAZ2; JAZ6; JAZ7; JAZ13; GAME9 functional cooperation on promoters; DELLA physical interaction
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: basic helix-loop-helix transcription factor with JID domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: leaf-expressed; induced by PAC, combined PAC+MeJA, and in pro after MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of MYC1 lowers α-tomatine and disables PAC-mediated SGA enhancement; also increases GA1 and hypocotyl elongation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive regulator and probable key DELLA-responsive MYC for SGA production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutant and RNAi lines; metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; Y2H; split-LUC; dual-luciferase transactivation; GA quantification; growth phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. myc1 and MYC1-RNAi plants had reduced α-tomatine. 2. MYC1 activated SGA, GA-catabolism and DELLA promoters, but repressed SMT1 and GA20ox2. 3. PAC failed to elevate α-tomatine in myc1, and DELLA interacted with MYC1, implicating MYC1 in JA-GA crosstalk.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: JAZ1; JAZ2; JAZ5? no; JAZ6; JAZ7; JAZ13; GAME9; DELLA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: MYC1 promotes defence chemistry at expense of growth; loss increases elongation and alters phytosterols
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA; PAC; GA treatment; Botrytis cinerea challenge
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native uprGAME11; uprJAZ2; uprGA2ox5; uprGA2ox7; uprDELLA; uprSMT1; uprGA20ox2 tested
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value regulatory target for balancing defence SGAs and growth; tissue-specific or inducible tuning may reduce growth penalty
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics and metabolomics in study; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence suggests MYC1 is more critical than MYC2 for DELLA-mediated SGA control.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; interaction with JAZ repressors and DELLA
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SGA biosynthesis; JA feedback; GA catabolism/signalling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GAME4; GAME11; JAZ2; JAZ6; GA2ox5; GA2ox7; DELLA
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SGA biosynthetic genes and GA catabolism/signalling genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: JA signalling relieved from JAZ repression; DELLA modulation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: negative feedback via induction of JAZ2 and JAZ6
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts with many JAZ proteins; cooperates with GAME9 on SGA promoters; interacts with DELLA
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: basic helix-loop-helix transcription factor with JID domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: leaf-expressed; induced by MeJA and combined PAC+MeJA; induced in pro with MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MYC2 positively regulates α-tomatine; overexpression increases SGA and lowers active GA, whereas RNAi reduces SGA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive activator of JA-dependent SGA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi and overexpression lines; metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; Y2H; split-LUC; dual-luciferase transactivation; GA quantification; growth phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC2-RNAi reduced α-tomatine, while MYC2-OE increased it. 2. MYC2 activated GAME4, GAME11, JAZ2, JAZ6, GA2ox5, GA2ox7 and DELLA-associated promoter outputs. 3. MYC2-OE reduced GA1/GA4 and growth, linking JA defence regulation to GA suppression.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: JAZ1; JAZ2; JAZ3; JAZ5; JAZ6; JAZ7; JAZ13; GAME9; DELLA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced MYC2 activity elevates defence metabolites but suppresses growth through reduced active GA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA; PAC; GA treatment; Botrytis cinerea challenge
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native uprGAME4; uprGAME11; uprJAZ2; uprJAZ6; uprGA2ox5; uprGA2ox7; uprDELLA tested
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target to induce SGA defence, but constitutive overexpression likely causes growth penalty; inducible deployment preferable
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq evidence cited from prior study; in this paper proteomics/metabolomics without accession
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts partly redundantly with MYC1 but shows target specialization, especially on GAME4 and JAZ6 promoters.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation in cooperation with MYC factors
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GAME4; GAME11 and other SGA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: co-activates SGA biosynthetic promoters with MYC1/MYC2
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: JA signalling context; MYC-associated regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: functional cooperation with MYC1 and MYC2 on promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: reduced in jar1; not induced in gid1TRI
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GAME9 supports activation of SGA biosynthetic genes together with MYCs
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important co-regulator of SGA pathway rather than sole master switch in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR association; transient transactivation assays with promoter reporters
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME9 transcript was reduced in jar1. 2. GAME9 with MYC2 activated uprGAME4. 3. GAME9 with MYC1 or MYC2 activated uprGAME11, indicating cooperative promoter control.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising stacking partner with MYC factors to enhance SGA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper functionally tests GAME9 cooperation in Results, not merely as background.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: GA perception promoting DELLA inactivation and thereby suppressing SGA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: growth–defence balance and SGA biosynthesis repression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DELLA pathway and indirectly JA/SGA genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: negative effect on SGA biosynthetic and JA-signalling gene expression via GA perception
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: GID1-DELLA interaction; GID1a used as positive control in Y2H
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of all three GID1 receptors elevates α-tomatine about two-fold and enhances resistance to Botrytis cinerea
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major negative controller of SGA accumulation through GA signalling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: triple CRISPR mutant analysis; LC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; proteomics; pathogen assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gid1TRI accumulated about double α-tomatine relative to WT. 2. Many JA, sterol and SGA biosynthetic genes were induced in gid1TRI. 3. gid1TRI showed smaller Botrytis lesions, linking GA perception loss to stronger defence chemistry.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA perception deficiency; Botrytis cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates GA receptor pathway as leverage point for defence enhancement, but full loss causes excessive dwarfing
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Triple mutant evidence shows GA perception normally suppresses SGA pathway.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chemical defence against pathogens and herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction modulation of MYC/JAZ activity; transcriptionally induced by MYC1/MYC2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-GA crosstalk controlling SGA biosynthesis and growth
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYC1; MYC2; JAZ7; indirectly SGA and GA-catabolism genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes MYC transcriptional output by attenuating JAZ repression rather than directly binding target promoters in this study
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GA via GID1 receptors; MYC1 and MYC2 transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of JA-to-GA feedforward control because MYCs induce DELLA, which can reinforce MYC activity against JAZ repression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: physically interacts with JAZ7, MYC1 and MYC2; modulates JAZ1/JAZ2 repression of MYC-driven promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: uprDELLA activated by MYC1 and MYC2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function pro mutant has reduced α-tomatine and altered JA responsiveness, showing DELLA promotes SGA accumulation under low-GA conditions
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key integrator of GA antagonism over JA-driven SGA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: analysis of pro mutant; Y2H; transactivation assays; hormone-treatment interaction experiments; pathogen assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pro mutant displayed reduced α-tomatine and greater susceptibility to Botrytis. 2. DELLA interacted with MYC1 and MYC2 and with JAZ7. 3. DELLA alleviated JAZ1/JAZ2 repression of MYC-driven GA2ox5 activation, explaining how low GA promotes SGA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High DELLA promotes defence chemistry but restrains growth; low DELLA supports growth but weakens defence
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PAC; GA; MeJA; Botrytis cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central leverage node for rebalancing growth and defence; partial/tissue-specific tuning preferable to avoid severe dwarfism
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tomato contains a single DELLA gene, PROCERA.
